subject: Re: Machine Stamp on Particle Stacks
body:
`mrcfile` from ccp-em throws an error by default when loading the files, 
but permissive=True turns it into a warning; though I'd rather not run in 
permissive mode, its not a critical bug.

On Monday, July 8, 2019 at 12:05:00 PM UTC-7, Dimitry Tegunov wrote:
>
> You're absolutely right, first byte should be 0x44. Will be fixed in the 
> next release. What software does this bug currently break?
>
>
> On Monday, July 8, 2019 at 8:56:48 PM UTC+2, Benjamin Barad wrote:
>>
>> The machine stamp on the particle stack mrcs files is `0x43 0x41 0x00 
>> 0x00`, which is unrecognized by the mrcfile library and doesn't seem to 
>> match the options I can find for the mrc2014 spec. Should the machine stamp 
>> be `0x44 0x41 0x00 0x00` (one of the options I found for little-endian 
>> data), or is there a reason that the specific machine stamp is written?
>>
>
